refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the strict latency requirement (p95 ≤ 1.0 s), the overall update process must be decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition and data processing separately, enabling targeted design solutions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure latency separately for data acquisition from sensors and telemetry, and for data processing and model update, ensuring combined latency meets the p95 ≤ 1.0 s criterion.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry protocols and optimized sensor data streams
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms with parallelization and incremental updates
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency respectively

potential_sources: Telemetry data acquisition tightly coupled with processing algorithms due to data format dependencies
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized to minimize cross-dependencies; incremental processing reduces latency coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of high-bandwidth telemetry infrastructure
Risk of processing bottlenecks if data volume spikes unexpectedly
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and processing load; consider fallback mechanisms for degraded network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry acquisition, data processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and vehicle data streams for worksite updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between vehicles, teleoperation, and central processing

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Limited containment; vehicle provides raw data but does not process updates.
Primarily consumes updated worksite model; minimal role in update latency.
Safety alerts are downstream consumers; no direct role in update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functions within NGHE Solution module optimizes latency and reduces interface overhead.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if NGHE Solution module is overloaded
Complexity in NGHE Solution may increase integration risk
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modular subcomponents within NGHE Solution to isolate acquisition and processing for scalability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR decomposition into acquisition and processing is clear and maps well to candidate DPs addressing latency.

diffusion_risks: Tight coupling between telemetry acquisition and processing may cause diffusion if not well modularized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning both DPs to NGHE Solution module maximizes containment and minimizes diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end and per sub-function within NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of update latency responsibility within NGHE Solution module.

recommended_revisions: Define internal submodules within NGHE Solution for acquisition and processing to manage complexity.
Establish performance monitoring interfaces to detect latency degradation.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and mapping to design and implementation modules is sufficient and meets latency requirement focus with clear ownership and verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs further if required.

assumptions: Telemetry infrastructure supports required bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can be optimized for real-time performance
open_risks: Network congestion or failures impacting telemetry latency
Processing load spikes causing latency violations
evidence_needed: Processing latency benchmarks under realistic workloads
